title: Fluffy Festive British Bread Fritters
description: Fermented, airy bread fritters with a splash of rice wine and a zesty tadka, perfect for a festive British snack.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine flour, yeast, sugar, and salt.
Add warm oat milk and rice wine, stirring until a soft dough forms.
Cover the bowl with a damp cloth and let the dough rise at room temperature for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Heat coconut oil in a deep skillet over medium heat.
Drop spoonfuls of the risen dough into the hot oil, flattening slightly with the back of a spoon.
Fry each fritter for 2–3 minutes per side or until golden brown and puffed.
Remove from oil and drain on paper towels. Sprinkle with a tadka of cumin, mustard seeds, and a pinch of grated ginger if desired.
Garnish with chopped cilantro before serving.
Serve hot with a side of mint yogurt dip or a simple salad.
